summary: A surprisingly clear 1968 audience tape that captures a hot show highlighted by Dark Star, the China Cat/Eleven sandwich and rare Born‑Cross‑Eyed moments despite typical AUD grain.
review: This recording delivers an energetic 1968 performance with standout jams – Dark Star, The Other One, China Cat into Eleven, Spanish Jam and a rare Born‑Cross‑Eyed – all delivered with the raw intimacy of an audience tape.  While the sound isn’t studio‑grade, reviewers repeatedly note it is remarkably clear for its era, with only occasional muffiness and a muted Pigpen keyboard in spots.

ai_rating.rationale: 19 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars; musical performance is praised heavily while recording quality, though not perfect, is considered good to very good for a 1968 AUD source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Surprisingly clear AUD tape for 1968; occasional murky sections and source‑switch around 2:34, muted Pigpen keyboards in parts, but overall listenable with decent instrument separation.

poor_songs: Lovelight (later part runs out of steam / split awkwardly)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical late‑’68 flow with long, exploratory jams; the China Cat → Eleven sandwich is a highlight despite a rough transition, and the rare Born‑Cross‑Eyed/Spanish Jam combo adds historic value.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his distinctive solo style (different from ’66), strong Dark Star improvisations, and lively banter that gets the audience involved.
band_member_comments.Bob: Comments range from praise of his vocal energy on Dancin' in the Streets to criticism of his drunken state during that number; also mention that his keyboard is muted for part of the tape.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ/keyboard is reported as muted in sections, a common issue with early AUD recordings; otherwise keyboards sit well in the mix.
